name: Naan
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the dry ingredients: flour, sugar, baking powder, salt, and baking soda. Stir until well mixed.
2. Make a well in the center of the dry mixture and add the eggs. Mix the eggs into the dry ingredients.
3. Gradually stir in milk until all ingredients are combined and the dough forms a soft, smooth ball. If the dough is too sticky, sprinkle a little additional flour over it.
4. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for several minutes until smooth and elastic.
5. Shape the dough into a large ball and place in a greased bowl. Cover with a towel and let rise in a warm place for 3 hours, or until doubled in size.
6. Preheat two ungreased baking sheets to 450°F (230°C).
7. Punch down the risen dough and divide it into six equal portions.
8. Roll each portion into a teardrop-shaped leaf that is about 6 inches long and 3 1/2 inches wide at its widest point, tapering to a thickness of 3/8 inch.
9. Place the naan leaves on the prepared baking sheets.
10. Bake for 6 minutes or until firm to the touch. Broil briefly (if desired) to lightly brown the tops.
11. Serve hot or at room temperature.
